title: Geolocated footage shows Ukrainian FPV drone strike destroying Russian ammunition depot near Kreminna
summary: WarTV shared geolocated combat footage of a Ukrainian FPV drone striking and destroying a Russian forward ammunition storage site near Kreminna in Luhansk Oblast, triggering sustained secondary explosions.
contents:
# Geolocated Footage: Ukrainian Drone Destroys Russian Ammunition Depot Near Kreminna

## Combat Footage Analysis
WarTV published geolocated video showing a Ukrainian first-person-view (FPV) drone striking a Russian ammunition storage position near Kreminna, Luhansk Oblast.

## Strike Details
- **Location**: Kreminna area, Luhansk Oblast
- **Geolocation**: Confirmed via terrain matching, approximately 48.97°N, 38.21°E
- **Target**: Forward ammunition storage site
- **Weapon**: FPV drone with high-explosive payload
- **Outcome**: Direct hit triggering massive secondary explosions lasting several minutes
- **Timestamp**: May 1, 2026, approximately 1245 local time

## Damage Assessment
- Primary explosion followed by sustained secondary detonations
- Fire and smoke column visible from multiple kilometers
- Storage site appeared to contain artillery shells and rocket ammunition
- Complete destruction of stored munitions probable

## Tactical Significance
- Loss of forward ammunition reserves degrades Russian artillery sustainment in the Kreminna sector
- Demonstrates continued effectiveness of Ukrainian drone operations against logistics targets
- Russian forces face lengthened supply lines from rear depots

## Source Attribution
Original footage sourced from Ukrainian 92nd Assault Brigade Telegram channel.
